exemplar.title:
Preparedness for earthquakes in San Rafael and Escazu, Costa Rica.
To what extent do demographic factors determine how well prepared the communities of Escazu and San Rafael are in the event of a major earthquake?

detailedExemplarData.title:
Preparedness for earthquakes in San Rafael and Escazu, Costa Rica.
To what extent do demographic factors determine how well prepared the communities of Escazu and San Rafael are in the event of a major earthquake?

subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

strengths: Research question clearly geographical and explicitly linked to IB Option D, enabling hypothesis formulation
Precise geographic context with wellâjustified site selection and feasibility for primary data
Highâquality locator and site maps following cartographic conventions
Three testable, theoryâdriven hypotheses clearly formulated
weaknesses: Lacks precise geographic coordinates or grid references for EscazÃº and San Rafael
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands meet the highest band: the question is strongly theoretical, context is specific and feasible, and mapping is conventionâcompliant. A minor omission of coordinates does not undermine full achievement of Criterion A.
feedback.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

strengths: Methods are clearly described with pilot testing and a comprehensive point system
Data collection log is detailed (dates, times, sites), demonstrating replicability
Mapping of survey locations uses appropriate symbols and scales
weaknesses: Reliance on convenience sampling introduces bias that is not fully critiqued
Cartographic figures lack a north arrow and coordinate grid
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The methodology is relevant and correctly implemented, yielding usable primary data, but descriptions of reliability checks and sampling limitations remain underdeveloped, placing this work squarely in the mid band (2 marks).

strengths: Large, directly relevant dataset (240 cases) supporting all hypotheses
Appropriate use of tables, bar graphs, scatter plots, boxplots and maps
Effective integration of spatial and statistical presentation in combined figures
weaknesses: Some table categories are blank and missingâdata handling is only briefly noted
Cramped labels, faint legends and occasional inconsistencies in figure numbering
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data are relevant and mostly sufficient, presentation techniques are generally strong but marred by labelling and convention errors, and conventions have occasional lapses. Overall performance aligns with the upperâmiddle band (4/6).
feedback.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

strengths: Use of appropriate statistical tests (t-tests, Spearmanâs rank, chi-square) with clear presentation of formulae and p-values
Patterns and anomalies are linked to hypotheses and socio-economic theory
Wellâstructured contingency tables and boxplot summary tables support interpretation
weaknesses: No confidence intervals, error bars or critical test values provided
Analysis remains descriptive in places with limited discussion of samplingâbias effects
Boxplots lack individual outlier markers and methodological footnotes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While statistical techniques and interpretation are solid, gaps in depth (e.g., missing intervals, bias discussion) and occasional descriptive passages place the work at the top of the 3â4 band (4/8) rather than into the higher band.

strengths: Conclusion clearly restates findings and explicitly states which hypotheses are accepted or rejected
Logical consistency maintained with direct support from analysis and tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is concise, fully supported by the analysis, and meets all descriptors for the top band (2/2).

strengths: Evaluation lists pertinent methodological limitations with explanation of their importance
Improvement suggestions are realistic and linked to potential impacts
weaknesses: Discussion lacks depth on how each limitation specifically skewed results
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strengths and weaknesses are appropriately outlined and improvements are relevant, but the evaluation does not reach the comprehensive depth required for full marks, aligning with the mid band (2/3).

strandSelections.reasoning: Two clear locator maps and additional site-specific maps (data-collection points) are provided with keys, scale bars and legible symbols, meeting accepted cartographic conventions.  Spatial representation is therefore accurate and purposeful.

The student poses a clearly geographical question focused on earthquake preparedness and demographic determinants.  It is explicitly linked to Option D of the IB syllabus and is followed by three testable hypotheses, demonstrating that the question provides a sound framework for hypothesis formation and theoretical discussion.

Tables, comparative bar graphs, scatter plots, boxplots and maps are all appropriate and generally effective.  While powerful statistical outputs are integrated, some figures suffer from cramped labelling or faint legends, suggesting strong but not best-possible presentation.

Surveys were administered correctly in Spanish, 120 responses were gained and locations are listed, showing appropriate technique use.  Yet reliance on convenience sampling and acknowledged issues with homeless respondents suggest only moderate data reliability, fitting the 2-mark descriptor rather than the highest band.

The analysis combines descriptive statistics with t-tests, Spearmanâs rank and chi-square, all appropriate to variable types.  Formulae and p-values are shown, indicating solid statistical competence, but no confidence intervals or error bars are provided, so the work fits comfortably in the 5-6 band at 5 marks.

Patterns (higher LoP in EscazÃº, lack of correlation with household size, higher LoP in older group) are clearly related back to hypotheses and socioeconomic theory.  Some anomalies are noted, yet a few links to broader hazard-vulnerability concepts are under-developed, so a solid but not exceptional 5 is justified.

The geographic context is precise: EscazÃº and San Rafael are located, their tectonic setting is outlined and feasibility for primary surveys is justified (urban proximity, similar seismic intensity).  Specific reasons for site selection and demographic contrasts are articulated, fully satisfying the highest descriptor.

A structured evaluation lists several pertinent methodological weaknesses (sampling bias, limited sites, bracket questions) and explains why they matter, but the discussion lacks depth on how each weakness might have skewed specific results, fitting the 2-mark descriptor.

A large, directly relevant primary data set (240 cases for household variables) is supplied and supports all three hypotheses.  Minor gaps (e.g., few cases for 6+ households) prevent a claim of full sufficiency, so the upper-middle band is most appropriate.

The conclusion restates key statistical findings and clearly indicates which hypotheses were accepted or rejected, directly supported by referenced tables and tests.  Logical consistency is maintained, warranting full marks in this criterion.

All three hypotheses are tested and discussed; nevertheless, sections remain descriptive in places and occasional gaps (e.g., limited exploration of sampling bias effects on results) reduce overall completeness.  This matches the upper-middle band (3-4) at 4 marks.

Methods (32-item survey, pilot study, point system, convenience sampling) are described and are relevant to the question; however, explanations of sampling bias, reliability checks and theoretical alignment are fairly brief.  The description is clear but not fully comprehensive, aligning with the middle band.

Figure numbering inconsistencies and occasional missing units (e.g., income symbols) occur, yet most axes, legends and titles are present.  These âoccasionalâ rather than âfrequentâ errors match the 3-mark position within the 3-4 band.

For every stated limitation the student proposes a realistic improvement and often links it to potential impact (e.g., wider spatial coverage to represent whole canton).  The suggestions are relevant though sometimes brief, corresponding to the middle band for this strand.

annotations.comment: Class thresholds for High/Medium/Low LoP are not defined in situ; include a legend or footnote clarifying these ranges.
The research question is clearly geographical, explicitly linked to IB Option D, and structured to allow hypothesis testing through theoretical constructs.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: While the tectonic setting is well explained, the write-up lacks precise geographic coordinates or site descriptors for EscazÃº and San Rafael; integrating these would strengthen locational context.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: Consider adding latitude/longitude or descriptive site markers when introducing locational context to improve spatial specificity.
Figure 1 is well adapted with a clear legend and scale, effectively depicting tectonic boundaries in Costa Rica.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: Include a north arrow and grid reference on Figure 1 to fully comply with cartographic conventions and aid map reading.
The three hypotheses are clearly formulated and justified, demonstrating strong theoretical linkage and testability.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: Null hypotheses lack consistent subscript formatting (e.g., Hâ); ensure statistical notation follows standard conventions for clarity.
The survey methodology is described with clear relevance to the research question, including pilot testing and development of a preparedness score.
The pointâsystem table is comprehensive, linking demographic and preparedness variables effectively to the LoP metric.
Several categories in the pointâsystem table remain blank (e.g., household size); populate all fields or explain omissions to ensure data sufficiency.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: The data collection log outlines dates, times, and sites in detail, demonstrating transparency and replicability in the sampling process.
Convenience sampling is described but its limitations (e.g., selfâselection bias) should be critically assessed in the methodology section.
Figures 5 and 6 follow mapping conventions with clear symbols and scale, accurately showing data collection sites in both cantones.
Add a north arrow and coordinate grid to Figures 5 and 6 to fully comply with cartographic best practices.
Table 3 presents directly relevant primary data, with means and medians supporting hypothesis testing effectively.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: The treatment of four missing responses is only noted briefly; discuss how these omitÂ­ted data were handled statistically to maintain data integrity.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Average monthly income calculations in Tables 4 and 5 are transparent, showing the method of weighted averaging by bracket.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Bar charts demonstrating income vs. LoP suffer from crowded legends and faint colour differentiation; enhance label clarity and colour contrast.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Figure 7 combines maps and tables to illustrate income disparities clearly, integrating statistical and spatial presentation effectively.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Split large combined figures into separate panels or supplement with summary tables to improve readability of complex spatial-statistical presentations.
The Spearmanâs rank test computation is clearly shown with covariance and R value, demonstrating appropriate statistical application.
Include the critical value for Spearmanâs test and clarify the significance threshold in the table to support the interpretation of R.
The scatter plot (Figure 10) lacks error bars or confidence intervals; adding these would deepen the statistical rigor of the analysis.
The contingency table for the chi-square test is well structured, including row and column totals for independence testing.
Chi-square results report pâvalue and degrees of freedom, but omitting the critical ÏÂ² value; include it to justify statistical decisions.
Data for both age groups is tabulated with sample sizes and summary statistics, facilitating direct comparison.
Consider segmenting ageâgroup data by canton to reveal spatial differences in LoP between sites, enriching analysis depth.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Boxplots lack individual data markers for outliers; overlaying jittered points would improve depiction of spread and anomalies.
Boxplot summary table (Table 9) succinctly reports IQR, boundaries, median, and mean, supporting interpretation of distribution.
Include notes on how whiskers and outliers are defined in boxplot summaries to avoid ambiguity in interpretation.
Evaluation outlines pertinent methodological limitations and realistic enhancements, linking improvements to potential impacts.
The evaluation would benefit from deeper analysis of how each limitation specifically biased results; connect each point to actual data effects.
